# Design System PR A: Token Pipeline + Figma Sync + Migration

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Retire `app/css/_tokens.css`, ship a two-tier (primitives + semantic) token pipeline generated by Style Dictionary, mechanically migrate all 31 `.module.css` files to semantic tokens with zero visual drift, and stand up bidirectional Figma Tokens sync infrastructure.

**Architecture:** JSON token sources under `design-system/tokens/*.json` become the single source of truth. Style Dictionary (exact-pinned v4) transforms them to `design-system/dist/{tokens.css,tokens.ts,tokens.json,tokens.figma.json}` at `predev`/`prebuild` time (gitignored outputs). A `postcss-value-parser` AST-based codemod (`scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs`) rewrites all 31 `.module.css` files using the finite legacy→semantic rename map from spec §3.3. Six new CI gates (token build/check, boundary lint, no-magic-values lint, contrast check, theme contract check, Figma drift) enforce the architecture. Two GitHub Actions (`tokens-figma-sync.yml`, `tokens-figma-pull.yml`) move tokens between this repo and a side repo (`erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync`) that the Tokens Studio Figma plugin reads from; code is always the source of truth and Figma changes land as reviewable PRs.

**Tech Stack:** Style Dictionary v4 (exact-pinned), postcss-value-parser, ts-morph (deferred to PR C), pnpm, vitest, GitHub Actions, fine-grained GitHub App token for cross-repo Figma sync.

---

## File Structure

### New files

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `design-system/tokens/color.json` | Primitive color ramps (`--ds-green-*`, `--ds-text-*`, `--ds-neutral-*`, `--ds-accent-*`, `--ds-feedback-*`) + semantic color aliases (`--ds-color-signal*`, `--ds-color-text-*`, `--ds-color-surface-*`, `--ds-color-border-*`, `--ds-color-feedback-*`, `--ds-color-highlight-*`, `--ds-color-accent-*`) per spec §3.2 and §3.3 |
| `design-system/tokens/space.json` | Primitive space scale `--ds-space-1`..`--ds-space-16` (4-64px linear) + semantic `--ds-space-pad`, `--ds-space-pad-tight`, `--ds-space-rhythm`, `--ds-space-rhythm-tight` |
| `design-system/tokens/typography.json` | Primitive sizes (`--ds-text-size-2xs`..`--ds-text-size-3xl`, 9 sizes), leadings, font stacks + semantic `--ds-font-size-body`, `--ds-font-size-heading-{sm,md,lg,xl}`, `--ds-font-family-{mono,display}`, plus full size aliases |
| `design-system/tokens/motion.json` | Primitive `--ds-duration-{fast,base,slow}`, `--ds-ease-{out,in,in-out}` + semantic `--ds-motion-{fade-default,press,reveal,shimmer}` (shorthand `duration easing`) |
| `design-system/tokens/layer.json` | Primitive `--ds-layer-{base,sticky,overlay,headline}` (no semantic layer in v1 per §3.4) |
| `design-system/tokens/border.json` | Primitive `--ds-radius-none`, `--ds-radius-sharp` and `--ds-border-width-1` (no semantic layer in v1 per §3.4) |
| `design-system/tokens/themes/crt-green.json` | Canonical theme semantic→primitive override map; **empty in PR A** (default mappings live in semantic tokens above); populated when PR D adds amber alongside it |
| `design-system/sd.config.ts` | Style Dictionary v4 config: source globs, platforms (css/ts/json/figma), custom transforms for W3C reference syntax (`{color.green.500}` → `var(--ds-green-500)`) |
| `design-system/dist/.gitkeep` | Keeps the gitignored dist directory present so `tokens:build` has an output dir on fresh clone |
| `design-system/dist/README.md` | One-paragraph "GENERATED — do not edit. Run `pnpm tokens:build`. Source: `design-system/tokens/`." banner |
| `design-system/.figma-sync-state.json` | Last-synced `tokens.figma.json` SHA + ISO timestamp; written by `tokens:figma:export`, read by `scripts/check-figma-token-drift.mjs` |
| `scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs` | postcss-value-parser AST codemod that rewrites every legacy CSS variable (`--signal`, `--fg`, etc.) to its semantic equivalent across all 31 `.module.css` files |
| `scripts/migrate-tokens.fixtures/before.css` | Codemod self-test input fixture: covers raw `var(--signal)`, nested `calc(var(--pad) + 4px)`, `color-mix(in srgb, var(--signal) 50%, transparent)`, `linear-gradient(var(--signal-dim), var(--bg))`, `@keyframes` referring to primitives |
| `scripts/migrate-tokens.fixtures/after.css` | Expected codemod output for the fixture above; diffed in `scripts/migrate-tokens.test.mjs` |
| `scripts/migrate-tokens.test.mjs` | Vitest test: feeds fixture to codemod, asserts equality with `after.css`, asserts no legacy tokens remain |
| `scripts/lint-token-boundary.mjs` | CI gate: scans every `.module.css` under `components/`, `app/`, `design-system/components/` for primitive `var()` references that have a semantic layer per spec §3.4; rejects with file:line:col |
| `scripts/lint-no-magic-values.mjs` | CI gate: scans every `.module.css` for raw hex/px/ms/z-index outside the allowlist per spec §3.5 |
| `scripts/lint-no-magic-values.allowlist.json` | Pre-populated allowlist of every legitimate magic value with `{file, value, justification}` per entry; built by Task 8 |
| `scripts/contrast-check.mjs` | CI gate: computes WCAG AA contrast for every semantic text/surface pair per spec §3.6; fails on any pair below threshold |
| `scripts/check-theme-contract.mjs` | CI gate: asserts every semantic token defined by the default tier is present in every theme file under `design-system/tokens/themes/`; runs the contrast check per theme |
| `scripts/check-figma-token-drift.mjs` | CI gate: regenerates `dist/tokens.figma.json` and diffs against `.figma-sync-state.json`; fails on drift with the canonical resolution steps in stderr |
| `scripts/diff-baselines.mjs` | Manual-review gate script: for each Playwright baseline that changed, emits a side-by-side ASCII summary + writes `playwright-report/baseline-diffs/<name>.png`; CI does NOT run this — human runs locally before commit |
| `.github/workflows/tokens-figma-sync.yml` | On push to `main`, regenerate `tokens.figma.json` and push to `erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync` (code → Figma direction) |
| `.github/workflows/tokens-figma-pull.yml` | On dispatch from the Figma sync repo (via `repository_dispatch`), open a PR with updated `design-system/tokens/*.json` files (Figma → code direction) |

### Modified files

| Path | Change |
|---|---|
| `app/globals.css` | Replace `@import "./css/_tokens.css";` with `@import "../design-system/dist/tokens.css";` |
| `app/css/_tokens.css` | **Deleted** (see Task 14) |
| All 31 `.module.css` files (see codemod target list in Task 7) | Rewritten by codemod to use semantic tokens only |
| `package.json` | Add scripts `tokens:build`, `tokens:check`, `tokens:figma:export`, `tokens:figma:check`, `predev`, `prebuild`, `lint:tokens`, `lint:magic-values`, `contrast:check`, `theme:check`, `figma:drift:check`, `migrate:tokens`; add exact-pinned deps `style-dictionary@<exact>`, `postcss-value-parser@<exact>` |
| `.gitignore` | Append `design-system/dist/` (keep `.gitkeep` and `README.md` tracked via negation `!design-system/dist/.gitkeep`, `!design-system/dist/README.md`) |
| `.github/workflows/ci.yml` | Add six new gate steps after the existing Biome step (token build, boundary lint, magic-values lint, contrast, theme contract, Figma drift); insert before the build step |
| `tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots/` | Regenerated baselines committed after manual review via `scripts/diff-baselines.mjs` (Task 17) |
| `DECISIONS.md` | Append ADR for the token pipeline + Figma sync + deletion of `_tokens.css` |
| `ARCHITECTURE.md` | Add §"Design system token pipeline" pointing to `design-system/` |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Update "Stack" line on CSS tokens; add boundary lint to gate list |

## Tasks

### Task 1: thinking-inversion — enumerate the class-of-bugs PR A introduces

**Files:**
- Create: `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-design-system-tokenized/pr-a-inversion.md`

This task produces no production code. It enumerates every failure mode from spec §8 that applies to PR A and binds each to the test or gate that catches it. Every row becomes a test case in later tasks.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the inversion document**

Write `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-design-system-tokenized/pr-a-inversion.md` with exactly this table (sourced from spec §8 cross-cutting failure modes that touch PR A):

| # | Failure mode | Test/gate that catches it | Owning task |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Codemod typo: a legacy name is rewritten to a misspelled semantic (e.g., `--ds-color-signel`) | Codemod self-test fixture diff; boundary lint rejects unknown `--ds-*` names | T6 fixture; T9 boundary lint |
| 2 | Orphan reference: a `.module.css` still says `--signal` after codemod runs | Ripgrep CI gate `lint:tokens` greps for legacy names outside `scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs` | T9 boundary lint |
| 3 | Primitive leakage into a component: `Button.module.css` uses `var(--ds-green-500)` directly | Boundary lint (`scripts/lint-token-boundary.mjs`) | T9 |
| 4 | Hardcoded magic value: `padding: 16px` instead of `var(--ds-space-pad-tight)` | `scripts/lint-no-magic-values.mjs` + pre-populated allowlist | T10, T11 |
| 5 | Contrast regression: a semantic remap silently shifts a text/surface pair below WCAG AA | `scripts/contrast-check.mjs` + Lighthouse | T12 |
| 6 | Visual drift on rename: a token rewrites to a 1px-different resolved value (e.g., `--fs-base: 14px` → `--ds-font-size-body: 16px` because of a JSON typo) | Playwright visual baselines + `diff-baselines.mjs` human review | T17, T18 |
| 7 | Style Dictionary version drift: a minor bump changes output format mid-development | Exact-pinned `style-dictionary` (no caret) + `tokens:check` regenerate+diff in CI | T3 install, T4 sd.config |
| 8 | Codemod misses tokens inside `calc()` / `color-mix()` / `linear-gradient()` wrappers because regex doesn't enter the wrapper | postcss-value-parser AST traversal in codemod; fixture covers all three wrappers + nested cases | T5, T6 |
| 9 | Visual baseline regeneration masks real drift: baselines auto-regen and pass without anyone looking | `diff-baselines.mjs` is the manual gate — human must run it locally and confirm intent before commit | T17 |
| 10 | `design-system/dist/*` missing on fresh clone: developer runs `pnpm dev`, gets import error from `globals.css` | `predev` lifecycle hook runs `tokens:build` automatically; gitignored dir kept present via `.gitkeep` | T15 |
| 11 | Allowlist not pre-populated: first lint run produces hundreds of "violations" and the lint gets disabled to merge | Allowlist pre-populated as part of PR A, every entry justified one-line; lint clean (zero unallowlisted) before merge | T11 |
| 12 | Figma sync conflict: designer pushes from Figma while engineer pushes from code; both repos diverge | Both directions land as PRs in their respective repos; **code repo always wins as source of truth**; Figma push reconciles on next `tokens-figma-pull.yml` run; documented in workflow comments | T19, T20 |
| 13 | Style Dictionary's W3C reference syntax doesn't match what Tokens Studio plugin expects | Custom Style Dictionary transform in `sd.config.ts` emits the exact `{color.green.500}` reference shape Tokens Studio v0.6+ consumes; verified against a captured Tokens Studio export snapshot in `scripts/migrate-tokens.fixtures/tokens.figma.reference.json` | T4 |
| 14 | Motion primitive leakage: `Hero.module.css` uses `transition: opacity var(--ds-duration-base) var(--ds-ease-out)` instead of `var(--ds-motion-fade-default)` | Boundary lint extended for motion primitives; `@keyframes` blocks are the documented exception (regex-detected scope) | T9 |
| 15 | GitHub Action uses a broad PAT that leaks repo-write access if compromised | Workflows use a **fine-grained GitHub App token** (`DESIGN_TOKENS_SYNC_APP_TOKEN`) scoped to `Contents: read+write` and `Pull requests: read+write` on `erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync` ONLY; documented in workflow `permissions` block and in DECISIONS.md | T19, T20 |

- [ ] **Step 2: Commit the inversion document**

```bash
git add docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-23-design-system-tokenized/pr-a-inversion.md
git commit -m "docs(design-system): pr-a thinking-inversion table"
```

```bash
touch design-system/dist/.gitkeep
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write the dist banner README**

Write `design-system/dist/README.md` with exactly:

```markdown
# design-system/dist — GENERATED

Do not edit any file in this directory by hand.

Source of truth: `design-system/tokens/*.json`.
Regenerate with: `pnpm tokens:build`.
CI gate: `pnpm tokens:check` (regenerates and diffs against committed; fails on drift).

See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-23-design-system-tokenized/design.md` §3.7.
```

- [ ] **Step 3: Update `.gitignore` to ignore dist outputs but track the placeholders**

Append to `.gitignore`:

```
# design-system: dist is generated by Style Dictionary on predev/prebuild
design-system/dist/*
!design-system/dist/.gitkeep
!design-system/dist/README.md
```

Run: `git check-ignore -v design-system/dist/tokens.css`
Expected: prints `.gitignore:<line>:design-system/dist/*` — confirms `tokens.css` will be ignored when generated.

Run: `git check-ignore -v design-system/dist/README.md`
Expected: exit code 1, no output — confirms README is tracked.

- [ ] **Step 5: code-review:code-review**

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the staged diff (`.gitignore` change + new files). Address any findings before commit.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

### Task 3: Install exact-pinned deps and add no-op `tokens:build` script

**Files:**
- Modify: `package.json`
- Modify: `pnpm-lock.yaml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Install Style Dictionary and postcss-value-parser exact-pinned**

Run: `pnpm add -D -E style-dictionary postcss-value-parser`
Expected: lockfile updated; `package.json` shows `"style-dictionary": "X.Y.Z"` and `"postcss-value-parser": "X.Y.Z"` with NO caret prefix.

- [ ] **Step 2: Verify exact pinning passes the existing gate**

Run: `pnpm check:dep-pinning`
Expected: PASS (the project's existing dep-pinning script already enforces no-caret for exact-pin opt-ins; verify both new deps are not flagged).

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the script entries (no-op until Task 4 lands `sd.config.ts`)**

Add to `package.json` `"scripts"` block (insert after `"bundle-check"`):

```json
"tokens:build": "style-dictionary build --config design-system/sd.config.ts",
"tokens:check": "pnpm tokens:build && git diff --exit-code -- design-system/dist/",
"tokens:figma:export": "node scripts/write-figma-sync-state.mjs",
"tokens:figma:check": "node scripts/check-figma-token-drift.mjs",
"lint:tokens": "node scripts/lint-token-boundary.mjs",
"lint:magic-values": "node scripts/lint-no-magic-values.mjs",
"contrast:check": "node scripts/contrast-check.mjs",
"theme:check": "node scripts/check-theme-contract.mjs",
"figma:drift:check": "node scripts/check-figma-token-drift.mjs",
"migrate:tokens": "node scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs",
```

Also add the `predev` and `prebuild` lifecycle hooks (npm runs these automatically before `dev`/`build`):

```json
"predev": "test -f design-system/dist/tokens.css || pnpm tokens:build",
"prebuild": "pnpm tokens:build",
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Smoke-check that lifecycle hook syntax parses**

Run: `node -e "JSON.parse(require('node:fs').readFileSync('package.json','utf8'))"`
Expected: exits 0, no output (valid JSON).

- [ ] **Step 5: code-review:code-review**

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the staged diff. Confirm scripts are exact strings, no caret leaks into the dep block.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit**

```bash
git add package.json pnpm-lock.yaml
git commit -m "build(design-system): install style-dictionary + postcss-value-parser, add tokens scripts and predev/prebuild hooks"
```

### Task 4: Author the token JSON files + Style Dictionary config

**Files:**
- Create: `design-system/tokens/color.json`
- Create: `design-system/tokens/space.json`
- Create: `design-system/tokens/typography.json`
- Create: `design-system/tokens/motion.json`
- Create: `design-system/tokens/layer.json`
- Create: `design-system/tokens/border.json`
- Create: `design-system/tokens/themes/crt-green.json`
- Create: `design-system/sd.config.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Author `color.json`**

Author primitives + semantic aliases per spec §3.2 and §3.3 in Style Dictionary v4 DTCG format (`$value` / `$type`). Required token names (every name MUST match the spec exactly):

Primitives:
- `--ds-green-50` `#0a1f0d`
- `--ds-green-100` `rgba(0,255,65,0.1)`
- `--ds-green-150` `rgba(0,255,65,0.12)`
- `--ds-green-300` `rgba(0,255,65,0.2)`
- `--ds-green-400` `rgba(0,255,65,0.4)`
- `--ds-green-500` `#00FF41`
- `--ds-green-700` `#0a8a2a`
- `--ds-text-100` `#E6FFE6`
- `--ds-text-300` `#5AE07B`
- `--ds-neutral-0` `#000000`
- `--ds-neutral-50` `#050505`
- `--ds-accent-amber` `#FFD86B`
- `--ds-accent-cyan` `#7FE4FF`
- `--ds-feedback-error` `#FF8A8A`

Semantic aliases (all use DTCG reference syntax `{green.500}`, transformed by sd.config.ts to `var(--ds-green-500)`):
- `--ds-color-signal` → `{green.500}`
- `--ds-color-signal-subtle` → `{green.400}`
- `--ds-color-signal-quiet` → `{green.100}`
- `--ds-color-signal-faint` → `{green.150}`
- `--ds-color-text-body` → `{text.100}`
- `--ds-color-text-muted` → `{green.400}`
- `--ds-color-text-faint` → `{text.300}`
- `--ds-color-surface-base` → `{neutral.0}`
- `--ds-color-surface-shell` → `{neutral.50}`
- `--ds-color-border-default` → `{green.400}`
- `--ds-color-feedback-error` → `{feedback.error}`
- `--ds-color-accent-warm` → `{accent.amber}`
- `--ds-color-accent-cool` → `{accent.cyan}`
- `--ds-color-highlight-bg` → `{green.500}`
- `--ds-color-highlight-fg` → `{neutral.0}`

(Cross-check: the existing `--fg` is `#c8facc`, but spec §3.3 maps `--ds-color-text-body` → `--ds-text-100` which the spec defines as `#E6FFE6`. The spec is the source of truth; use `#E6FFE6`. If Task 17 visual baselines surface drift on body text, escalate — do not silently revert to `#c8facc`.)

- [ ] **Step 2: Author `space.json`**

Primitives `--ds-space-1` (4px) through `--ds-space-16` (64px) — exact linear scale every 4px: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, 40, 44, 48, 52, 56, 60, 64.

Semantics:
- `--ds-space-pad` → `{space.6}` (24px)
- `--ds-space-pad-tight` → `{space.3}` (12px)
- `--ds-space-rhythm` → `{space.16}` (64px)
- `--ds-space-rhythm-tight` → `{space.4}` (16px)
- `--ds-layout-maxw` → `1200px` (raw, no primitive)

- [ ] **Step 3: Author `typography.json`**

Primitive sizes:
- `--ds-text-size-2xs` 9px
- `--ds-text-size-xs` 11px
- `--ds-text-size-sm` 12px
- `--ds-text-size-base` 14px
- `--ds-text-size-md` 16px
- `--ds-text-size-lg` 22px
- `--ds-text-size-xl` 32px
- `--ds-text-size-2xl` 48px
- `--ds-text-size-3xl` 78px

Primitive leadings: `--ds-text-leading-tight` 1.2, `--ds-text-leading-base` 1.55, `--ds-text-leading-relaxed` 1.85.

Primitive font stacks:
- `--ds-text-font-mono` `var(--font-mono), ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace`
- `--ds-text-font-display` `var(--font-display), ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif`

Semantic aliases:
- `--ds-font-size-body` → `{text.size.base}`
- `--ds-font-size-heading-sm` → `{text.size.lg}`
- `--ds-font-size-heading-md` → `{text.size.xl}`
- `--ds-font-size-heading-lg` → `{text.size.2xl}`
- `--ds-font-size-heading-xl` → `{text.size.3xl}`
- `--ds-font-size-2xs` through `--ds-font-size-3xl` (full size aliases per spec §3.3 note)
- `--ds-font-family-mono` → `{text.font.mono}`
- `--ds-font-family-display` → `{text.font.display}`

- [ ] **Step 4: Author `motion.json`**

Primitives:
- `--ds-duration-fast` 80ms
- `--ds-duration-base` 200ms
- `--ds-duration-slow` 300ms
- `--ds-ease-out` `cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`
- `--ds-ease-in` `cubic-bezier(0.7, 0, 0.84, 0)`
- `--ds-ease-in-out` `cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1)`

Semantic shorthand (consumed as `transition: opacity var(--ds-motion-fade-default)`):
- `--ds-motion-fade-default` → `200ms cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1)`
- `--ds-motion-press` → `80ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`
- `--ds-motion-reveal` → `300ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1)`
- `--ds-motion-shimmer` → `300ms cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1)`

Note: motion semantics are emitted as raw composite strings (sd.config.ts adds a `motion/shorthand` formatter that resolves the duration+easing references into a single value). Components consume the semantic; `@keyframes` blocks are the documented exception where primitives may be referenced directly (spec §3.4).

- [ ] **Step 5: Author `layer.json`**

Primitives only (no semantic layer in v1):
- `--ds-layer-base` 0
- `--ds-layer-sticky` 50
- `--ds-layer-overlay` 100
- `--ds-layer-headline` 150

- [ ] **Step 6: Author `border.json`**

Primitives only:
- `--ds-radius-none` 0
- `--ds-radius-sharp` 2px
- `--ds-border-width-1` 1px

- [ ] **Step 7: Author `themes/crt-green.json` (empty body in PR A)**

Write the file with the DTCG envelope and a `"$themes"` key referencing the existing semantic tokens; the file is structurally present but contains no overrides — overrides are added in PR D when `crt-amber.json` is introduced. This locks in the file's existence so `check-theme-contract.mjs` has something to iterate over.

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://design-tokens.org/schema.json",
  "$themes": ["crt-green"],
  "color": {}
}
```

- [ ] **Step 8: Author `design-system/sd.config.ts`**

The config must:
- Source: `design-system/tokens/**/*.json`
- Register a custom transform `reference/css-var` that converts DTCG references (`{color.green.500}`) to `var(--ds-green-500)` (the variable name format the boundary lint expects)
- Register a custom format `motion/shorthand` that resolves motion semantic tokens (which compose two references) into a single string
- Register a custom format `figma/w3c` that emits the W3C DTCG JSON shape Tokens Studio v0.6+ reads, with the `$value`/`$type` envelope (cross-check by diffing output against `scripts/migrate-tokens.fixtures/tokens.figma.reference.json`, an expected-shape snapshot)
- Platforms:
  - `css` → `design-system/dist/tokens.css` (selector `:root, [data-theme="crt-green"]`)
  - `ts` → `design-system/dist/tokens.ts` (typed const tree, includes literal-type `Tokens` export)
  - `json` → `design-system/dist/tokens.json` (flat key/value)
  - `figma` → `design-system/dist/tokens.figma.json` (W3C DTCG shape)
- Every output file emits a top-of-file banner: `/* GENERATED — do not edit. Source: design-system/tokens/. Run pnpm tokens:build. */`

- [ ] **Step 9: Run `pnpm tokens:build` and verify outputs exist**

Run: `pnpm tokens:build`
Expected: exit 0; `design-system/dist/{tokens.css,tokens.ts,tokens.json,tokens.figma.json}` all exist.

- [ ] **Step 10: Spot-check the generated CSS**

Run: `grep -E '^\s*--ds-color-signal:' design-system/dist/tokens.css`

Run: `grep -E '^\s*--ds-motion-fade-default:' design-system/dist/tokens.css`
Expected: `--ds-motion-fade-default: 200ms cubic-bezier(0.65, 0, 0.35, 1);` — motion semantics resolve to raw strings (not references) because CSS doesn't support composite-var references in `transition:` shorthand.

### Task 5: Codemod skeleton — load files, no-op rewrite

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the codemod skeleton with no rewrites yet**

Structure:
- Imports: `node:fs/promises`, `node:path`, `node:url`, `postcss-value-parser`, `fast-glob` (use the existing dep; if not present, install exact-pinned in this task and update Task 3's commit)
- Constants:
  - `TARGET_GLOBS = ['app/**/*.module.css', 'components/**/*.module.css', 'design-system/components/**/*.module.css']`
  - `LEGACY_TO_SEMANTIC` map — the finite rename table from spec §3.3 (mirror table below in Step 4)
- Main flow:
  1. Resolve every file matching `TARGET_GLOBS`
  2. For each file: read, parse each `var(...)` reference via `postcss-value-parser`, rewrite per the map, write back if changed
  3. Emit a summary: files scanned, files rewritten, total references rewritten
  4. Exit non-zero if any legacy name remains after the rewrite (sanity check)
- CLI flag `--dry-run`: print the diff to stdout without writing
- CLI flag `--fixture`: read input from stdin, write output to stdout (for the test in Task 6)

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the skeleton against the repo (expect zero rewrites because the map is empty)**

Run: `node scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs --dry-run`
Expected: exits 0, summary reports `files scanned: 31, files rewritten: 0`.

- [ ] **Step 3: Add the AST traversal — handle nested `var()` inside `calc()` / `color-mix()` / `linear-gradient()`**

Use `postcss-value-parser`'s `walk` API to recursively visit every `function` node. When the function name is `var` and the first child is a Word token starting with `--`, look up the name in `LEGACY_TO_SEMANTIC`. Rewrite in place. The walker naturally descends into `calc`, `color-mix`, `linear-gradient`, and any other wrapping function — this is the explicit defense against the regex-misses-wrappers bug from spec §8 row 8.

Pseudocode (no production code in the plan; the engineer writes the actual implementation against the fixture in Task 6):

```
parsed = valueParser(declarationValue)
parsed.walk(node => {
  if (node.type === 'function' && node.value === 'var') {
    const firstArg = node.nodes[0]
    if (firstArg?.type === 'word' && firstArg.value.startsWith('--')) {
      const replacement = LEGACY_TO_SEMANTIC[firstArg.value]
      if (replacement) firstArg.value = replacement
    }
  }
})
const rewritten = parsed.toString()
```

The codemod walks the entire stylesheet (not just declaration values): iterate every line, capture every `var(...)` regardless of context, including inside `@media` blocks, inside `@keyframes` blocks, and inside `@supports` blocks. **Inside `@keyframes` blocks, motion-primitive references are legal per spec §3.4 — the codemod still rewrites them because the legacy→semantic map only covers names that have a semantic equivalent.** Primitives without semantic equivalents (layer, radius, border, motion duration/easing inside `@keyframes`) are not in the map and pass through untouched.

- [ ] **Step 4: Populate `LEGACY_TO_SEMANTIC` with the finite rename map from spec §3.3**

The exact map (every entry comes from the spec §3.3 "Replaces (old name)" column):

```
'--signal'         → '--ds-color-signal'
'--signal-dim'     → '--ds-color-signal-subtle'
'--signal-dim-2'   → '--ds-color-signal-quiet'
'--signal-faint'   → '--ds-color-signal-faint'
'--fg'             → '--ds-color-text-body'
'--muted'          → '--ds-color-text-muted'
'--muted-dim'      → '--ds-color-text-faint'
'--bg'             → '--ds-color-surface-base'
'--shell-bg'       → '--ds-color-surface-shell'
'--border'         → '--ds-color-border-default'
'--error-soft'     → '--ds-color-feedback-error'
'--accent-warm'    → '--ds-color-accent-warm'
'--accent-cool'    → '--ds-color-accent-cool'
'--highlight-bg'   → '--ds-color-highlight-bg'
'--highlight-fg'   → '--ds-color-highlight-fg'
'--pad'            → '--ds-space-pad'
'--vrhythm'        → '--ds-space-rhythm'
'--fs-base'        → '--ds-font-size-body'
'--fs-lg'          → '--ds-font-size-heading-sm'
'--fs-xl'          → '--ds-font-size-heading-md'
'--fs-2xl'         → '--ds-font-size-heading-lg'
'--fs-3xl'         → '--ds-font-size-heading-xl'
'--fs-2xs'         → '--ds-font-size-2xs'
'--fs-xs'          → '--ds-font-size-xs'
'--fs-sm'          → '--ds-font-size-sm'
'--fs-md'          → '--ds-font-size-md'
'--font-mono-stack'    → '--ds-font-family-mono'
'--font-display-stack' → '--ds-font-family-display'
'--maxw'           → '--ds-layout-maxw'
```

**Mobile-override edge case:** the legacy CSS sets `--pad: 12px;` and `--vrhythm: 16px;` inside `@media (max-width: 768px)` to switch to the tight values. The codemod cannot rewrite a `--pad: 12px;` *declaration* to `--ds-space-pad-tight: ...` because the *role* changes (a redefinition vs an alias swap). **Codemod policy:** rewrite only `var(--pad)` consumption sites to `var(--ds-space-pad)`, and leave the redefinition `--pad: 12px;` declarations as legacy declarations. Task 14 (which deletes `_tokens.css`) reintroduces the mobile override by mapping `--ds-space-pad: var(--ds-space-pad-tight);` inside the `@media` block in `app/globals.css`. Document this carve-out as a one-line comment in `LEGACY_TO_SEMANTIC`.

**Pure tokens dropped:** `--red`, `--yellow`, `--green-light` are used only for chrome dots (traffic-light hexes); they have no semantic role in the design system. The codemod leaves them as-is in `.module.css`. They are migrated by Task 14 into a small `chrome-dots.css` partial that ships with `app/globals.css` — outside the token system on purpose.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run the codemod against the repo in `--dry-run` to see proposed changes (DO NOT WRITE YET)**

Run: `node scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs --dry-run | head -100`
Expected: a diff-style preview showing rewrites in Hero.module.css and others. Do NOT commit these changes — they're written in Task 7 after the fixture test in Task 6 proves the codemod is correct.

- [ ] **Step 6: code-review:code-review**

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the codemod source. Focus: AST traversal correctness, error handling on unparseable CSS, exit-code semantics.

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit (codemod only, no .module.css rewrites yet)**

```bash
git add scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs
git commit -m "feat(design-system): add postcss-value-parser AST codemod with legacy→semantic rename map"
```

Write `scripts/migrate-tokens.test.mjs` as a vitest test:
- `describe('migrate-tokens codemod')` with cases:
  1. `rewrites bare var(--legacy) to var(--semantic)`
  2. `rewrites var() nested inside calc()`
  3. `rewrites var() nested inside color-mix()`
  4. `rewrites var() nested inside linear-gradient()`
  5. `rewrites var() inside @media query body`
  6. `passes through var() inside @keyframes body when the name has no semantic equivalent`
  7. `passes through unknown var() names untouched`
  8. `leaves --pad: 12px; redefinition declarations untouched (mobile override carve-out)`
  9. `idempotent: running the codemod twice produces no further changes`

The fixture structure: read `before.css`, pipe through the codemod (`--fixture` flag, stdin/stdout), assert equality with `after.css`. Each case is a `:root + .test-{n}` block in the same fixture file so the AST sees realistic surrounding context.

- `linear-gradient(var(--signal-dim), var(--bg))`
- `@media (max-width: 768px) { .x { padding: var(--pad); --pad: 12px; } }` (consumption rewrites; redefinition does not)
- `@keyframes pulse { 0% { opacity: var(--ds-duration-base); } }` (no rewrite — `--ds-duration-base` is not in the legacy map)
- `var(--this-is-not-a-token)` (passes through)
- A nested case: `box-shadow: 0 0 4px var(--signal), 0 0 8px var(--signal-dim);`
- A `var()` with a fallback: `var(--signal, #00FF41)` — the fallback must be preserved as-is

Mirror `before.css` but with every legacy name swapped per `LEGACY_TO_SEMANTIC`. The redefinition `--pad: 12px` line stays as `--pad: 12px`. The `@keyframes` content stays untouched. The fallback `var(--signal, #00FF41)` becomes `var(--ds-color-signal, #00FF41)`.

### Task 9: Boundary lint — implementation

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/lint-token-boundary.mjs`

- [ ] **Step 1: Implement `lintFile(path)` and the CLI runner**

Behavior per spec §3.4:
- Use `postcss-value-parser` to find every `var()` reference in the file
- Track whether the current rule sits inside an `@keyframes` block (simple stateful scan, since the file is read line-by-line and `{...}` nesting is deterministic in CSS Modules)
- Rejected primitive prefixes (every match outside `@keyframes`):
  - `--ds-green-`, `--ds-text-` followed by a digit (numeric primitives, e.g., `--ds-text-100`), `--ds-neutral-`, `--ds-accent-`, `--ds-feedback-`
  - `--ds-space-` followed by a digit (numeric)
  - `--ds-text-size-`, `--ds-text-leading-`
  - `--ds-duration-`, `--ds-ease-`
- Inside `@keyframes`: only the motion primitives (`--ds-duration-*`, `--ds-ease-*`) are exempted; color/space/typography primitives still fail
- Allowed: any `var(--ds-color-*)`, `var(--ds-space-pad{,-tight})`, `var(--ds-space-rhythm{,-tight})`, `var(--ds-font-*)`, `var(--ds-layout-*)`, `var(--ds-motion-*)`, `var(--ds-layer-*)`, `var(--ds-radius-*)`, `var(--ds-border-*)`
- Allowed: also any `var(--ds-font-size-*)` semantic (the full alias scale per spec §3.3)
- Allowed: also legacy `var(--red)`, `var(--yellow)`, `var(--green-light)` (chrome-dot pure tokens, outside the design system per Task 5 Step 4 carve-out)
- Exception path: skip `design-system/dist/tokens.css` (primitives are the input there per spec §3.4)
- Output format on violation: `file:line:col [LEGACY_TOKEN|PRIMITIVE_LEAK|MOTION_LEAK] var(--name) — use var(--alternative) instead`
- CLI: walks the same globs as the codemod (`app/**/*.module.css`, `components/**/*.module.css`, `design-system/components/**/*.module.css`); exit 0 if zero violations, exit 1 otherwise

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the fixture test — expect PASS**

Run: `pnpm vitest run scripts/lint-token-boundary.test.mjs`
Expected: 4 passed.

- [ ] **Step 3: Run against the real repo — expect PASS now that Task 7 migrated everything**

Run: `pnpm lint:tokens`
Expected: exit 0, "boundary lint clean: 31 files, 0 violations".

If any violation surfaces, that's a Task 7 miss — fix the codemod, re-run Task 7, then re-run this step.

- [ ] **Step 4: Add legacy-name ripgrep gate to the same script (orphan-reference catch)**

Extend `lint-token-boundary.mjs` (or add a sibling `lint-legacy-tokens.mjs` called from the same npm script) to grep for any of the 29 legacy names from `LEGACY_TO_SEMANTIC` outside `scripts/migrate-tokens.mjs` and `scripts/migrate-tokens.fixtures/`. Exit 1 if found. The mobile-override carve-out (`--pad: 12px;` redefinitions inside `@media`) is grandfathered until Task 14 — the gate skips lines matching `^\s*--pad:\s*\d`.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run the orphan gate — expect PASS**

Run: `pnpm lint:tokens`
Expected: exit 0.

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the lint source + the new fixture rule for the orphan-reference grep.

Expected: FAIL.

- [ ] **Step 4: Implement `lint-no-magic-values.mjs`**

Per spec §3.5:
- Reject raw hex colors `#[0-9a-fA-F]{3,8}` outside `var()` arguments — must use a color token
- Reject raw `px` values outside specific allowlisted properties (`outline-offset`, `border-width`, sub-pixel calc tweaks, `text-shadow` blur radii) AND outside the allowlist file
- Reject raw `ms`/`s` durations (must use `--ds-duration-*` via a `var()` ref)
- Reject hardcoded z-index values (must use `--ds-layer-*`)
- Load `scripts/lint-no-magic-values.allowlist.json` — entries shaped `{ file: 'components/foo.module.css', line: 42, value: '9999px', kind: 'PX', justification: 'honeypot offset for sr-only field' }`. Match by `(file, line, value)` tuple
- Output: `file:line:col [HEX|PX|MS|ZINDEX] '<raw value>' — use a token or add to scripts/lint-no-magic-values.allowlist.json`

- [ ] **Step 5: Run the fixture test — expect PASS**

Run: `pnpm vitest run scripts/lint-no-magic-values.test.mjs`
Expected: 5 passed.

### Task 11: Populate the no-magic-values allowlist

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/lint-no-magic-values.allowlist.json`

- [ ] **Step 1: Run the lint with an empty allowlist to surface every violation**

```bash
echo '[]' > scripts/lint-no-magic-values.allowlist.json
pnpm lint:magic-values > /tmp/magic-values-violations.txt 2>&1 || true
wc -l /tmp/magic-values-violations.txt
```

Expected: a list of violations (dozens to ~100, per spec §3.5 note). Inspect with `cat /tmp/magic-values-violations.txt`.

- [ ] **Step 2: Triage each violation into one of three buckets**

For each violation, decide:
- **Bucket A — should be a token:** add a `// TODO` comment to the .module.css line and a follow-up entry in the task list below. Common cases: a px value that's actually in the scale but written raw → just rewrite to `var(--ds-space-N)`. A hex value that maps to an existing color token → rewrite. These DO NOT go in the allowlist.
- **Bucket B — legitimate exception:** add an allowlist entry with the file path, line number, raw value, and a one-sentence justification. Common cases: `9999px` honeypot offset for the sr-only contact-form trap field, sub-pixel border tweaks (`0.5px`) for hairline dividers on hi-dpi displays, `text-shadow` blur radii (`0 0 4px #000`) where the color is intentionally a raw black for legibility on top of the phosphor green.
- **Bucket C — pure-data scoped token:** chrome-dot hexes (`#ff5f57`, `#febc2e`, `#28c840`) — these are the canonical macOS traffic-light hexes and have no semantic role. Allowlist them with the justification "macOS traffic-light canonical hex; intentionally outside the design system."

- [ ] **Step 3: Fix every Bucket A violation by rewriting the .module.css line**

For each Bucket A site, edit the .module.css to use the correct semantic token. Re-run `pnpm lint:magic-values` after each batch.

- [ ] **Step 4: Write the populated `scripts/lint-no-magic-values.allowlist.json`**

The file structure (every entry documented per CLAUDE.md instruction in this plan):

```json
[
  {
    "file": "components/sections/Hero.module.css",
    "line": 64,
    "value": "#000",
    "kind": "HEX",
    "justification": "Raw black in text-shadow stack — intentionally not --ds-color-surface-base, the phosphor-green-on-black legibility halo requires a hard black value at this site."
  },
  {
    "file": "components/responsive/StatusBar.module.css",
    "line": "<line>",
    "value": "#ff5f57",
    "kind": "HEX",
    "justification": "macOS traffic-light canonical red; intentionally outside the design system per spec §3.2 chrome-dot carve-out."
  }
  // ... one entry per Bucket B/C violation
]
```

**Allowlist policy:** every entry MUST include `file`, `line`, `value`, `kind`, and a `justification` of at least one sentence explaining why a token isn't appropriate. Reviewers reject allowlist entries with placeholder or empty justifications.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run the lint — expect clean PASS**

Run: `pnpm lint:magic-values`
Expected: exit 0, "no-magic-values lint clean: 31 files, <N> allowlisted exceptions, 0 unallowlisted violations".

- [ ] **Step 6: code-review:code-review on the allowlist**

```
describe('contrast-check', () => {
  it('computes a 4.5:1+ ratio for body text on surface-base', async () => {
    const ratio = await ratioFor('--ds-color-text-body', '--ds-color-surface-base')
    expect(ratio).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(4.5)
  })
  it('computes a 3:1+ ratio for signal on surface-base (large text)', async () => {
    const ratio = await ratioFor('--ds-color-signal', '--ds-color-surface-base')
    expect(ratio).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(3.0)
  })
  it('checkAllPairs returns an empty failure list for crt-green', async () => {
    expect(await checkAllPairs('crt-green')).toEqual([])
  })
})
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run the test — expect FAIL (script not yet defined)**

Run: `pnpm vitest run scripts/contrast-check.test.mjs`
Expected: FAIL.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement `contrast-check.mjs`**

Behavior per spec §3.6:
- Read `design-system/dist/tokens.json` to resolve every token to its final color value
- Implement the WCAG relative-luminance + contrast-ratio formulas (no external dep — both are <30 lines)
- Hardcode the pair list from spec §3.6:
  - `(--ds-color-text-body, --ds-color-surface-base, 4.5)`
  - `(--ds-color-text-muted, --ds-color-surface-base, 4.5)`
  - `(--ds-color-text-faint, --ds-color-surface-base, 4.5)`
  - `(--ds-color-signal, --ds-color-surface-base, 3.0)`
  - `(--ds-color-text-body, --ds-color-surface-shell, 4.5)`
- Theme-aware: takes a `--theme` CLI arg (default `crt-green`); reads the theme's overrides on top of base tokens
- Output: per-pair table with `ratio | threshold | PASS|FAIL`; exit 1 if any FAIL

- [ ] **Step 4: Run the test — expect PASS**

Run: `pnpm vitest run scripts/contrast-check.test.mjs`
Expected: 3 passed.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run against the real tokens — expect PASS**

Run: `pnpm contrast:check`
Expected: all 5 pairs PASS for `crt-green`.

If any pair fails, that means the spec's chosen primitive values don't actually meet AA. **Stop and escalate** — do not silently bump the token to a brighter value. The semantic mapping in spec §3.3 is the design contract.

- [ ] **Step 6: code-review:code-review**

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the contrast formulas (math correctness matters here).

- [ ] **Step 7: Commit**

```bash

git commit -m "feat(design-system): WCAG AA contrast check for every semantic text/surface pair"
```

Contents:

```css
/* Chrome-dot canonical hexes + mobile pad/rhythm override.
   These three colors are macOS traffic-light hexes; they have no semantic role
   in the design system per spec §3.2. Kept out of the token pipeline by design. */
:root {
  --red: #ff5f57;
  --yellow: #febc2e;
  --green-light: #28c840;
}

/* Mobile override: tighten pad/rhythm by aliasing the canonical roles to the
   tight variants. Required because spec §3.3 defines pad-tight/rhythm-tight as
   distinct semantic tokens; consumers reference --ds-space-pad and the override
   swaps the alias at the breakpoint. */
@media (max-width: 768px) {
  :root {
    --ds-space-pad: var(--ds-space-pad-tight);
    --ds-space-rhythm: var(--ds-space-rhythm-tight);
  }
}
```

### Task 16: `diff-baselines.mjs` — the manual-review gate for visual regeneration

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/diff-baselines.mjs`

This script is the human's last line of defense against silent visual drift. It is NOT run in CI — it runs locally by the human before committing regenerated baselines.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the script**

Behavior:
- Inputs: two directories — `<old-baselines>` (the prior committed baselines, restored to a temp dir from `git show HEAD:...`) and `<new-baselines>` (the freshly-regenerated baselines from `playwright test --update-snapshots`)
- For each pair: compute a pixelmatch diff (use `pixelmatch` npm; install exact-pinned in this task), write a side-by-side `<name>__side-by-side.png` to `playwright-report/baseline-diffs/`, and print a one-line summary: `<name>: <changed-pixel-count> pixels (<pct>%)` sorted by largest delta first
- Print a final summary table; if any pair has >1% changed pixels, print a bold "INTENT REVIEW REQUIRED" warning and exit 1 (forcing the human to acknowledge)
- Add to package.json: `"baselines:diff": "node scripts/diff-baselines.mjs"`

- [ ] **Step 2: Smoke-test against the existing baselines (no regen yet)**

Run: `pnpm baselines:diff -- --old tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots --new tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots`
Expected: every file shows 0 pixels changed (comparing to itself).

- [ ] **Step 3: code-review:code-review**

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the script + the new dep.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/diff-baselines.mjs package.json pnpm-lock.yaml
git commit -m "feat(design-system): diff-baselines manual-review gate for visual regeneration"
```

### Task 17: Regenerate Playwright visual baselines + manual review via `diff-baselines.mjs`

**Files:**
- Modify: `tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots/*` (regenerated)

This task IS the human-in-the-loop gate. The agentic worker must pause here for Erik's review before committing.

- [ ] **Step 1: Snapshot the current baselines for diffing**

```bash
mkdir -p /tmp/baselines-before
cp -R tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots/* /tmp/baselines-before/
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Regenerate baselines via Playwright**

Run: `pnpm test:e2e --update-snapshots`
Expected: Playwright runs the full 4-project matrix, updates every snapshot under `tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots/`. (Existing CI workflow `update_visual_baselines` input runs this on Linux in GitHub Actions — see `ci.yml` — but for local PR-A development, regenerating locally on the dev machine is the path. The CI regeneration is for cross-platform pixel parity.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Run `diff-baselines.mjs` and ESCALATE if intent review required**

```bash
pnpm baselines:diff -- --old /tmp/baselines-before --new tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots
```

Expected: each updated baseline reports a pixel delta. Per spec §8, the expected delta is **zero or near-zero** — the codemod was mechanical, so the resolved CSS values should match exactly. If any baseline shows >1% changed pixels, **stop and escalate to Erik with the side-by-side PNG** — that means the token rename quietly changed a resolved value (e.g., body text `--fg: #c8facc` vs `--ds-text-100: #E6FFE6`).

If escalation is needed: the side-by-side PNGs are in `playwright-report/baseline-diffs/`. Recommend either (a) accept the intentional shift and document in DECISIONS.md, or (b) override the spec by reverting the token's primitive value to match the legacy value. Default recommendation: option (a) — the spec is the source of truth.

- [ ] **Step 4: Wait for human approval before committing**

Do NOT proceed to Step 5 without an explicit "approved" from Erik. This is a hard stop.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit the regenerated baselines**

```bash
git add tests/e2e/visual.spec.ts-snapshots
git commit -m "test(design-system): regenerate visual baselines after token migration (reviewed via diff-baselines.mjs)"
```

### Task 19: Figma sync — code → Figma workflow

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/tokens-figma-sync.yml`
- Create: `scripts/write-figma-sync-state.mjs` (used by `pnpm tokens:figma:export` after a successful sync)

**Security note (architect-reviewer concern):** This workflow uses a **fine-grained GitHub App token**, NOT a personal access token. The token is a GitHub App installation token for an App scoped specifically to `erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync`:
- Permissions: `Contents: read+write`, `Pull requests: read+write`
- Installation: only on `erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync` (not on the portfolio repo or any other repo)
- Secret name in this repo: `DESIGN_TOKENS_SYNC_APP_TOKEN`
- Token rotation: GitHub App tokens auto-expire in 1 hour; the workflow generates a fresh installation token on each run via `actions/create-github-app-token@v2` using `DS_SYNC_APP_ID` and `DS_SYNC_APP_PRIVATE_KEY` repo secrets

This is the correct security posture per spec §8 row 15 (security failure mode "GitHub Action uses a broad PAT"). A broad PAT would have read+write on every repo Erik owns; the App token cannot escape its scoped repo. Document this in DECISIONS.md as part of Task 22.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the workflow**

`.github/workflows/tokens-figma-sync.yml`:

```yaml
name: tokens-figma-sync (code → figma)

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - 'design-system/tokens/**'
      - 'design-system/sd.config.ts'
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read

concurrency:
  group: tokens-figma-sync
  cancel-in-progress: false  # never cancel a sync mid-flight

jobs:
  sync:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v6
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - run: pnpm tokens:build
      - name: Generate App installation token (fine-grained, sync-repo only)
        id: app-token
        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v2
        with:
          app-id: ${{ secrets.DS_SYNC_APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ secrets.DS_SYNC_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          owner: erikunha
          repositories: design-tokens-figma-sync
      - name: Push tokens.figma.json to sync repo
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          tmp=$(mktemp -d)
          git clone --depth 1 https://x-access-token:${GH_TOKEN}@github.com/erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync.git "$tmp"
          cp design-system/dist/tokens.figma.json "$tmp/tokens.figma.json"
          cd "$tmp"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          if git diff --quiet; then
            echo "No token changes to sync."
            exit 0
          fi
          git add tokens.figma.json
          git commit -m "chore(tokens): sync from erikunha/portfolio@${GITHUB_SHA::7}"
          git push origin main
      - name: Record sync state
        run: pnpm tokens:figma:export
      - name: Open PR back to portfolio with updated .figma-sync-state.json
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          if git diff --quiet design-system/.figma-sync-state.json; then
            echo "Sync state unchanged."
            exit 0
          fi
          branch="figma-sync-state/${GITHUB_SHA::7}"
          git checkout -b "$branch"
          git add design-system/.figma-sync-state.json
          git commit -m "chore(design-system): record figma sync state ${GITHUB_SHA::7}"
          git push origin "$branch"
          gh pr create --base main --head "$branch" \
            --title "chore(design-system): record figma sync state ${GITHUB_SHA::7}" \
            --body "Auto-opened after tokens-figma-sync.yml pushed tokens to erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync."
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write `scripts/write-figma-sync-state.mjs`**

Behavior:
- Compute the SHA256 of `design-system/dist/tokens.figma.json`
- Write `design-system/.figma-sync-state.json` with `{ sha256, syncedAt: <ISO timestamp>, source: 'code', commit: <GITHUB_SHA or HEAD> }`

- [ ] **Step 3: Local smoke-test the script**

Run: `pnpm tokens:build && pnpm tokens:figma:export && cat design-system/.figma-sync-state.json`
Expected: prints the JSON with a valid sha256 and timestamp.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit the script and state file**

```bash
git add scripts/write-figma-sync-state.mjs design-system/.figma-sync-state.json
git commit -m "feat(design-system): script to record figma sync state after export"
```

- [ ] **Step 5: code-review:code-review on the workflow**

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the workflow YAML. Focus: token scope (no broad PAT), concurrency, error handling on the git push, and the secondary PR-back step that records sync state.

- [ ] **Step 6: Commit the workflow**

```bash
git add .github/workflows/tokens-figma-sync.yml
git commit -m "ci(design-system): code→figma sync workflow using fine-grained app token"
```

---

### Task 20: Figma sync — Figma → code workflow + reconciliation policy

**Files:**
- Create: `.github/workflows/tokens-figma-pull.yml`

**Reconciliation policy (architect-reviewer concern):** When Figma and code push simultaneously, **the code repo always wins as source of truth**. The mechanism:
1. `tokens-figma-sync.yml` (Task 19) pushes code → Figma sync repo on every main push that touches tokens
2. `tokens-figma-pull.yml` (this task) opens a PR to the portfolio repo when the Figma sync repo changes (via `repository_dispatch` from a workflow in the sync repo, or via scheduled poll every 15 minutes)
3. The PR contains the Figma-side changes applied to `design-system/tokens/*.json`. **It does NOT auto-merge.** A human reviews and either accepts (Figma's change becomes canonical) or rejects (code-side state wins; the next code→Figma sync rolls back the Figma repo)
4. If both directions land overlapping changes: the code-side PR merges first (already in flight from a developer); the Figma-pull PR opens with a conflict marker that the human resolves in the PR UI

Document this policy in DECISIONS.md as part of Task 22, and as a comment block at the top of the workflow.

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the workflow**

`.github/workflows/tokens-figma-pull.yml`:

```yaml
name: tokens-figma-pull (figma → code)

# Reconciliation policy: code repo is source of truth. This workflow opens a PR
# with the Figma-side changes; a human reviews. If accepted, Figma's change
# becomes canonical. If rejected, the next code→figma sync run reverts the
# sync repo to match code.

on:
  repository_dispatch:
    types: [tokens-changed-in-sync-repo]
  schedule:
    - cron: '*/15 * * * *'  # poll every 15 min as fallback
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: write
  pull-requests: write

concurrency:
  group: tokens-figma-pull
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  pull:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v6
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v6
        with:
          node-version: 22
          cache: pnpm
      - run: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      - name: Generate App installation token
        id: app-token
        uses: actions/create-github-app-token@v2
        with:
          app-id: ${{ secrets.DS_SYNC_APP_ID }}
          private-key: ${{ secrets.DS_SYNC_APP_PRIVATE_KEY }}
          owner: erikunha
          repositories: design-tokens-figma-sync
      - name: Fetch latest tokens.figma.json from sync repo
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ steps.app-token.outputs.token }}
        run: |
          curl -sSL -H "Authorization: Bearer $GH_TOKEN" \
            -o /tmp/figma-tokens.json \
            https://raw.githubusercontent.com/erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync/main/tokens.figma.json
      - name: Compare with local synced state
        id: compare
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          incoming_sha=$(sha256sum /tmp/figma-tokens.json | awk '{print $1}')
          local_sha=$(jq -r '.sha256' design-system/.figma-sync-state.json)
          if [ "$incoming_sha" = "$local_sha" ]; then
            echo "No Figma-side drift; nothing to pull."
            echo "drift=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            exit 0
          fi
          echo "drift=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
      - name: Apply Figma-side changes to design-system/tokens/*.json
        if: steps.compare.outputs.drift == 'true'
        run: node scripts/apply-figma-pull.mjs /tmp/figma-tokens.json
      - name: Open PR with Figma-side proposal
        if: steps.compare.outputs.drift == 'true'
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          branch="figma-pull/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git checkout -b "$branch"
          git add design-system/tokens
          git commit -m "chore(design-system): proposal from figma sync (review before merging)"
          git push origin "$branch"
          gh pr create --base main --head "$branch" \
            --title "chore(design-system): proposal from figma sync" \
            --body "Auto-opened by tokens-figma-pull.yml. Designer pushed tokens from Figma; this PR applies the diff to design-system/tokens/*.json. **Reconciliation policy: code is source of truth — accept (merge) or reject (close; next code→figma run reverts sync repo).**"
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Write `scripts/apply-figma-pull.mjs`**

Behavior:
- Read the incoming `tokens.figma.json` (W3C DTCG shape)
- For each top-level category (color, space, typography, etc.), translate the DTCG entries back into the source JSON shape used in `design-system/tokens/*.json`
- Write the updated files
- Preserve any source-side comments/keys that Figma doesn't roundtrip (the W3C format is a subset)

Implementation note: this is the inverse of the `figma/w3c` Style Dictionary format. The translator must be 1:1 reversible — Task 4 Step 8 cross-checks this by diffing `code → figma → code` round-trip output against the original source files. Add a test for this round-trip in `scripts/migrate-tokens.test.mjs` (extend the existing test file).

### Task 21: Figma drift CI gate

**Files:**
- Create: `scripts/check-figma-token-drift.mjs`
- Modify: `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the failing test**

`scripts/check-figma-token-drift.test.mjs`:

```
describe('check-figma-token-drift', () => {
  it('passes when dist sha matches .figma-sync-state.json', async () => {
    expect(await checkDrift()).toEqual({ drift: false })
  })
  it('fails when dist sha differs from .figma-sync-state.json', async () => {
    // mock by temporarily tampering with the state file
    const tampered = await checkDriftWith({ stateSha: 'deadbeef' })
    expect(tampered.drift).toBe(true)
  })
})
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run — expect FAIL**

Run: `pnpm vitest run scripts/check-figma-token-drift.test.mjs`
Expected: FAIL.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement `check-figma-token-drift.mjs`**

Behavior per spec §3.10:
- Run `pnpm tokens:build` (or assume dist is fresh; CI orders this)
- Compute SHA256 of `design-system/dist/tokens.figma.json`
- Read `design-system/.figma-sync-state.json`
- Compare; if drift, exit 1 with stderr message: "Figma drift detected. Resolution: (a) if code changed, run `pnpm tokens:figma:export && git add design-system/.figma-sync-state.json && git commit`. (b) If Figma changed, wait for the next tokens-figma-pull.yml run."

- [ ] **Step 4: Run the test — expect PASS**

Run: `pnpm vitest run scripts/check-figma-token-drift.test.mjs`
Expected: 2 passed.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run against the real repo — expect PASS**

Run: `pnpm figma:drift:check`
Expected: PASS.

- [ ] **Step 6: Add the gate to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`**

Insert six new steps in the `build-and-gate` job, after the existing "Biome check" step, before the build step:

```yaml
      - name: Build design tokens
        run: pnpm tokens:build

      - name: Tokens drift check
        run: pnpm tokens:check

      - name: Token boundary lint
        run: pnpm lint:tokens

      - name: No-magic-values lint
        run: pnpm lint:magic-values

      - name: Contrast check
        run: pnpm contrast:check

      - name: Theme contract check
        run: pnpm theme:check

      - name: Figma drift check
        run: pnpm figma:drift:check
```

- [ ] **Step 7: code-review:code-review**

Invoke `code-review:code-review` on the CI changes + the new script. Confirm the gates are ordered correctly (tokens:build first, then everything that depends on dist).

- [ ] **Step 8: Commit**

```bash
git add scripts/check-figma-token-drift.mjs scripts/check-figma-token-drift.test.mjs .github/workflows/ci.yml
git commit -m "ci(design-system): figma drift gate + wire all six design-system gates into ci.yml"
```

- [ ] **Step 1: Append the ADR to `DECISIONS.md`**

Append an entry dated `2026-05-23` with:
- **Decision:** Adopted Style Dictionary v4 two-tier token pipeline (`design-system/tokens/*.json` → `design-system/dist/tokens.{css,ts,json,figma.json}`) replacing `app/css/_tokens.css`. All 31 `.module.css` files migrated to semantic tokens via postcss-value-parser AST codemod.
- **Context:** Spec at `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-23-design-system-tokenized/design.md`; spec §3 token architecture; spec §7.1 PR A scope.
- **Mechanism:** Six CI gates added (tokens:check, lint:tokens, lint:magic-values, contrast:check, theme:check, figma:drift:check); `predev`/`prebuild`/`pretest` lifecycle hooks; bidirectional Figma sync via `erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync` shared repo + fine-grained GitHub App token.
- **Reversibility:** Medium. Codemod is mechanical and idempotent; `git revert` + `pnpm tokens:build` reverses everything. Visual baselines may need a second regeneration cycle.
- **Notes:**
  - Reconciliation: code is source of truth; Figma changes land as PRs to this repo.
  - Fine-grained GitHub App token scoped to `Contents+Pull requests: read+write` on `erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync` only — NOT a broad PAT (security failure mode spec §8 row 15).
  - Chrome-dot canonical hexes (`--red`, `--yellow`, `--green-light`) intentionally kept out of the design system; live in `app/css/chrome-dots.css`.

### Task 23: Final verification + PR preparation

**Files:**
- No new files

- [ ] **Step 1: Run the full local CI gate**

Run: `pnpm verify`
Expected: every step PASS.

- [ ] **Step 2: Run `pnpm bundle-check` to confirm zero bundle delta on `/`**

Run: `pnpm build && pnpm bundle-check`
Expected: bundle-check reports no client-JS regression on `/` (this PR adds no client JS — only CSS-variable indirection).

- [ ] **Step 3: Run `pnpm test:e2e` against the regenerated baselines**

Run: `pnpm test:e2e`
Expected: all 4 projects (chromium/webkit × desktop/mobile) PASS.

- [ ] **Step 4: Local Playwright MCP visual check (per CLAUDE.md merge gate rule 8)**

Start `pnpm dev`; use Playwright MCP to load `/` at 1280×720 and 375×812; confirm visual parity by eye against the live production site. Particularly inspect: Hero CTAs, body text color (the `--fg #c8facc` → `--ds-text-100 #E6FFE6` shift surfaces here most visibly), section spacing, mobile pad/rhythm override.

If anything looks wrong, STOP and escalate to Erik before opening the PR.

- [ ] **Step 5: Run `pnpm ready-to-merge` (no PR number yet — this is dry-run validation)**

Run: `pnpm ready-to-merge`
Expected: ci:local + branch-protection check PASS. (Thread-resolution check requires a PR number; skip until the PR is opened.)

- [ ] **Step 6: Open the PR**

Use `commit-commands:commit-push-pr` skill to open the PR. The PR description must reference spec §7.1 by file path and call out:
- Six new CI gates
- 31 `.module.css` files migrated
- `_tokens.css` deleted
- Figma sync workflows added (sync repo `erikunha/design-tokens-figma-sync` exists with fine-grained App token configured)
- Visual baselines regenerated, manually reviewed via `diff-baselines.mjs`
- DECISIONS.md ADR + ARCHITECTURE.md + CLAUDE.md updated

- [ ] **Step 7: Wait for Copilot auto-review on PR open (per CLAUDE.md rule 7)**

Do NOT post any comment on the PR until Copilot has reviewed. After Copilot finishes:
1. Triage every comment per CLAUDE.md PR merge gate rule 2 (RESOLVE or ESCALATE — no third bucket)
2. Apply fixes as new commits (never amend per rule 1)
3. Reply to each thread with `Fixed in <sha>. <one-sentence reason>` per rule 7
4. Re-request Copilot review via `gh pr edit <pr> --add-reviewer copilot-pull-request-reviewer`
5. Repeat until zero open threads
6. Rebase onto main (`git fetch && git rebase origin/main`) per rule 9
7. Run `pnpm ready-to-merge <pr>` — must PASS before merge
8. Local Playwright MCP visual check on desktop + mobile (per rule 8) — required AFTER all review fixes are pushed
9. Merge
